target.title: chore(lint): enable no-underscore-dangle with comprehensive allow list
target.body:
## Summary

- Enable the `eslint/no-underscore-dangle` lint rule in `.oxlintrc.json` with a comprehensive allow list covering all existing underscore-prefixed identifiers across the codebase
- This replaces the implicit rule (previously unconfigured, causing 90+ lint errors) with an explicit error-level rule that enforces the convention on new code while allowing established patterns

Follows the direction of #81635 but expands the allow list to cover the full set of violations discovered by running `npx oxlint src/ extensions/`, bringing the error count from 90+ to 0.

Part of the #80504 lint cleanup plan.

## Verification

### Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: lint rule enablement, no runtime behavior change
Real environment tested: Linux, Node v22
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
npx oxlint src/ extensions/
```
Evidence after fix:
```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 2.1s on 13530 files with 188 rules using 8 threads.
```
Observed result after fix: All lint errors eliminated. The rule now enforces the underscore convention on new identifiers while allowing the 170+ established patterns.

What was not tested: full `pnpm check`; CI will validate remaining lanes.
